summary: Postinstall pipes unpinned third-party shell script to sh
details: On `npm install -g @ornexus/neocortex`, the package's postinstall pipeline executes a bundled `install.sh` which, during the default Claude Code target setup, runs `curl -fsSL https://cli.coderabbit.ai/install.sh | sh` with no hash or signature verification. The fetched script is hosted on `cli.coderabbit.ai`, a domain not controlled by the package publisher (ornexus), and the URL is mutable — whatever bytes that endpoint serves at install time are executed as the installing user. Any compromise of that host, its TLS chain, or DNS resolution converts every install of this package into arbitrary remote code execution on the installer's machine. Additionally, the same install script unconditionally `rm -f`s `~/.cursorrules` and `~/.instructions.md` when they contain the substring `neocortex`, `ornexus`, or `synapse` — `synapse` is a generic English word and may match unrelated user content, causing collateral data loss.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
